evidence: we continue to make investments in initiatives focused on the further development and deployment of these technologies
We have integrated AI capabilities into several of our solutions and provide our associates with access to internal AI tools
We leverage AI to unlock new commercial opportunities and enhance overall productivity
we have introduced a number of AI-driven solutions such as BondGPT and OpsGPT with agentic capabilities while continuing to develop new AI applications

We offer an integrated, modern open-architecture wealth management platform through which we provide enhanced data-centric capabilities, integrating the use of AI, to improve the overall client experience
Our AI Academy offers voluntary learning paths and courses for our associates to establish a robust foundation in AI

use of AI in our product initiatives and offerings or services...may give rise to risks related to accuracy, bias, discrimination, intellectual property infringement, misappropriation or leakage of proprietary, confidential and personal information

Any error, defect, or vulnerability in our AI-powered products or business processes could undermine the quality of our products and services, adversely impact our clients businesses, subject us or our clients to regulatory scrutiny, fines or litigation
The absence of a unified regulatory framework, and the risk of divergent or conflicting regulations across jurisdictions...could increase the complexity and costs of compliance for us and our clients

limitations: AI investment intensity scored 3 (not 4) because no dollar amounts are explicitly ring-fenced as AI-specific capex; data center and cloud spend figures are for broader IT infrastructure, not AI-dedicated spend

Narrative concreteness scored 4 because BondGPT and OpsGPT are named deployed products with operational detail, but no quantified business results (e.g., revenue, time saved, volume) are disclosed in the excerpts

Risk disclosure scored 4 because specific risk mechanisms (bias, IP infringement, regulatory fragmentation, client harm) are named with operational impact pathways, but no quantified risk exposure or named governance/oversight body is disclosed
